clinical_study.brief_title: Erdafitinib Plus Cetrelimab in FGFR-Altered Platinum-Refractory Urothelial Carcinoma (ERDACEL-UC)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Single-Arm Trial of Erdafitinib (Pan-FGFR Inhibitor) Combined With Cetrelimab (Anti-PD-1) in Patients With FGFR2 or FGFR3-Altered Platinum-Refractory Locally Advanced or Metastatic Urothelial Carcinoma Who Are Receiving or Have Received Erdafitinib Monotherapy

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates the safety and efficacy of adding cetrelimab (an investigational anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody) to ongoing erdafitinib therapy in patients with FGFR2- or FGFR3-altered platinum-refractory urothelial carcinoma (UC) who are currently on erdafitinib and have achieved at least stable disease. The hypothesis is that PD-1 blockade can overcome adaptive immune resistance induced by FGFR inhibition-driven immunosuppression, potentially converting stable disease to objective response and prolonging response duration.

detailed_description.textblock: FGFR3 mutations and FGFR2/3 fusions occur in approximately 20% of muscle-invasive and metastatic urothelial carcinoma. Erdafitinib, a pan-FGFR inhibitor, is FDA-approved for FGFR2/3-altered platinum-refractory UC (THOR trial). However, responses are often partial and durable complete responses are uncommon. FGFR inhibition has complex effects on the tumor immune microenvironment: preclinical data suggest FGF signaling promotes an immunosuppressive milieu by excluding T cells, and FGFR inhibition may restore immune competence. Cetrelimab is an investigational fully human IgG4 anti-PD-1 antibody with demonstrated monotherapy activity in UC. This single-arm Phase 2 study enrolls FGFR-altered platinum-refractory UC patients currently receiving or initiating erdafitinib who have demonstrated at least stable disease after 2 cycles (approximately 8 weeks). Cetrelimab 375 mg IV Q3W is added to ongoing erdafitinib 8 mg/day (with potential dose-escalation to 9 mg/day per FGF23/phosphate monitoring per standard protocol). Primary endpoint is ORR per RECIST 1.1 by independent central review. This cisplatin-ineligible, platinum-refractory population represents a high unmet need given limited options after FGFR inhibitor therapy.

primary_outcome.measure: Objective Response Rate (ORR) per RECIST 1.1 by blinded independent central review (BICR)
primary_outcome.time_frame: Best overall response from combination start, assessed every 9 weeks up to 24 months

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From first objective response to progression or death, up to 24 months
From initiation of cetrelimab until progression or death, up to 24 months
From enrollment until death from any cause, up to 36 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Disease Control Rate (DCR): CR + PR + SD lasting >= 16 weeks

secondary_outcome.time_frame: During treatment period, up to 24 months

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed urothelial carcinoma (transitional cell carcinoma) of the bladder, urethra, ureter, or renal pelvis; mixed histologies with predominant urothelial component (>= 50%) are eligible - Locally advanced (cT4b or unresectable) or metastatic disease (N2-3 or M1); Stage IVA or IVB per AJCC 8th edition - FGFR2 or FGFR3 alteration confirmed by a validated assay (NGS-based panel or Therascreen FGFR RGQ RT-PCR Kit): FGFR3 mutations (S249C, R248C, Y373C, G370C, A393E, or others listed in erdafitinib label); FGFR2 or FGFR3 fusions; FGFR2 amplification with confirmed oncogenic significance - Currently receiving erdafitinib monotherapy and has achieved at least stable disease (SD) per RECIST 1.1 after a minimum of 2 cycles (approximately 6-8 weeks) of erdafitinib; patients newly initiating erdafitinib may be enrolled and assessed for SD eligibility during a 2-cycle run-in - Prior platinum-based chemotherapy: must have received at least one prior platinum-containing regimen (cisplatin or carboplatin-based) for locally advanced or metastatic UC, or in the neoadjuvant/adjuvant setting with recurrence within 12 months - Cisplatin-ineligible status documented at time of platinum-containing regimen (GFR < 60 mL/min, ECOG PS >= 2, hearing loss Grade >= 2, CTCAE neuropathy Grade >= 2, or NYHA Class III heart failure) — cisplatin-ineligible patients who received carboplatin-based regimens are eligible - May have received prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy (pembrolizumab, atezolizumab, durvalumab) for UC; washout of >= 6 weeks required - May have received prior enfortumab vedotin or sacituzumab govitecan; washout of >= 2 weeks required - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-2 - At least one measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1 (target lesion >= 1 cm in longest diameter by CT, or >= 1.5 cm in short axis for lymph nodes) - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1000/mm3, platelets >= 75,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 8 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN (or <= 5x ULN with liver metastases) - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 20 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault); note: erdafitinib can be administered with reduced GFR; cetrelimab dose adjustment not required - Serum phosphate and calcium within institutional normal limits at baseline (FGFR inhibitor-related hyperphosphatemia management per erdafitinib label; baseline phosphate < 5.5 mg/dL required prior to combination initiation) - Ophthalmologic evaluation (slit-lamp exam) within 28 days of enrollment; central serous retinopathy (CSR) must be resolved or Grade <= 1 prior to combination - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Non-urothelial primary tumor (pure squamous cell carcinoma, pure adenocarcinoma, small cell carcinoma, or sarcomatoid carcinoma without urothelial component) - FGFR1 or FGFR4 alterations only (without FGFR2 or FGFR3 co-alteration); FGFR3 point mutations not listed in the erdafitinib prescribing information require sponsor consultation for eligibility - Disease progression on erdafitinib prior to enrollment (PD as best response to erdafitinib excludes the patient; SD or better required) - Uncontrolled or symptomatic CNS metastases; treated and stable brain metastases are allowed if neurologically stable for >= 4 weeks and off corticosteroids - Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy that would preclude anti-PD-1 use - Prior Grade 3-4 immune-mediated adverse event on anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy (except endocrinopathies managed with replacement therapy) - Active or unresolved Grade >= 2 ocular toxicity (central serous retinopathy, corneal changes) attributable to erdafitinib; patients must have ophthalmologic clearance prior to adding cetrelimab - Uncontrolled hyperphosphatemia (serum phosphate >= 5.5 mg/dL) at time of enrollment; dietary phosphate restriction and/or phosphate binder initiation required prior to enrollment - Active hepatitis B (HBsAg positive without antiviral prophylaxis) or active hepatitis C - Live vaccine within 30 days prior to enrollment - Known hypersensitivity to erdafitinib or cetrelimab - Concurrent participation in another interventional trial - Pregnancy or breastfeeding; adequate contraception required - ECOG Performance Status >= 3

intervention.description: Erdafitinib 8 mg orally once daily continuously (with potential dose-escalation to 9 mg/day at day 14-21 if serum phosphate < 5.5 mg/dL per standard pharmacodynamic-guided dosing), plus cetrelimab 375 mg IV every 3 weeks (21-day cycles), until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or patient withdrawal. Both agents administered concurrently.
